title: Tilray Brands Surges on Record $207 Million Q3 Revenue: Margin Recovery in Beverages and German Cannabis Growth Signal Turnaround
text: Tilray Brands reported record fiscal 2026 Q3 net revenue of $206.7 million, representing an 11% year-over-year increase. The company achieved adjusted earnings-per-share of $0.02, surpassing analyst expectations for a loss. Adjusted EBITDA jumped 19% to $10.7 million, while the net loss narrowed dramatically to $25.2 million from $793.5 million in the prior year period. The International Cannabis segment emerged as a standout performer, with revenue surging 73% year-over-year to $24.1 million. Germany drove much of this expansion, as regulatory changes expanded the medical cannabis patient base. Management noted that German medical flower sales doubled in volume, establishing Tilray's dominant European position. In Canada, Tilray maintained number one market share ranking despite industry-wide price pressures, with combined medical and adult-use revenue growing 8% to approximately $40 million. The beverages division generated $42.6 million in revenue through deliberate strategic shifts. The company executed aggressive brand rationalization, eliminating underperforming labels to emphasize higher-margin craft and THC-infused beverages. Management characterized the 32% gross margin as a hard floor, with expectations for improvement as new partnerships scale. Tilray aims to reach $1 billion in annual revenue through balanced cannabis and diversified consumer packaged goods operations.
